pi_items.verified_value: OpenICE (Open-source Integrated Clinical Environment) - real medical device interoperability platform developed by MD PnP at Massachusetts General Hospital
pi_items.source: Web search confirmed OpenICE is published at openice.info and github.com/mdpnp/mdpnp. Environment documentation at evidence_docs/README.md identifies OpenICE as 'medical device interoperability platform developed by MD PnP'

pi_items.verified_value: IEEE 11073 standard medical device nomenclature codes - MDC_ECG_HEART_RATE (147842 decimal, 0x24182 hex) for cardiac heart rate, MDC_PULS_OXIM_SAT_O2 for pulse oximetry oxygen saturation
pi_items.source: IEEE 11073 standard confirmed via web search. MDC codes are standardized international nomenclature for point-of-care devices including ECG and pulse oximeters

pi_items.verified_value: synthetic - generated by OpenICE simulated medical devices (Simulated Multiparameter Monitor, Simulated Pulse Oximeter)
pi_items.source: Task description explicitly states 'create simulated devices' and export_result.sh lines 86-87 search for 'Simulated Multiparameter Monitor' and 'Simulated Pulse Oximeter' in logs

privileged_info_summary: This clinical data ETL pipeline task uses OpenICE, a real open-source medical device interoperability platform developed by MD PnP at Massachusetts General Hospital. The task generates synthetic vital signs data from simulated medical devices (Simulated Multiparameter Monitor and Simulated Pulse Oximeter) that emit standard IEEE 11073 metric codes including MDC_ECG_HEART_RATE (heart rate) and MDC_PULS_OXIM_SAT_O2 (oxygen saturation). The agent must extract and parse these simulated metrics from OpenICE logs located at /home/ga/openice/logs/openice.log and output a CSV file to /home/ga/Desktop/vital_signs_dataset.csv with headers 'Timestamp,Metric,Value'. The verification requires a minimum of 30 rows with both HeartRate and SpO2 metrics present.
